creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #15)

Bad interpretation on my part. Trying again . . .

Well, armc's are in use that are like (per the messages
that show up only for the failure cases so far):

7156a000
7156b000
7156c000
7156d000
7156e000
7156f000
71570000

Note how all of those fit in 2 GiBytes starting at 0x0.

Value comments added for symbolic names:

static struct bcm283x_memory_mapping bcm2838_memmap[] = {
        {
                /* SDRAM */
                .armc_start = 0x00000000,
                .armc_size = 0x40000000,
                .vcbus_start = BCM2838_VCBUS_SDRAM_BASE, // 0xC0000000
        },
        {
                /* Main peripherals */
                .armc_start = BCM2838_ARM_IO_BASE,    // 0xfe000000
                .armc_size = BCM28XX_ARM_IO_SIZE,     // 0x01000000
                .vcbus_start = BCM2838_VCBUS_IO_BASE, // 0x7E000000
        },
        { 0, 0, 0 },
};

Looks to me like bcm2838_memmap only deals with the armc_starts used
for 4 GiByte and 8 GiByte RPi4B's. The 2 GiByte ones use address that
fit in the lower 2 GiByte area starting at 0x0.

The 1 GiByte RPi4B's likely have the analogous issue for addresses with
3??????? instead of 7???????, staying inside the 1 GiByte address range
based at 0x0.

The vcbus_start does seem to be invariant across memory sizes, however.
So not finding the likes of 7156a000 in Main peripherals ends up not
doing the required adjustment to vcbus space addressing. And that
leads to the failures via use of incorrect vcbus addresses.

(I'm not looking at the other direction for translation for now.)
